pre_founder.text: Welcome to Reindustrialize. You are about to launch a manufacturing company of your own, starting with an almost-empty garage and one chance to prove the shop can deliver.
Every manufacturing powerhouse starts with small decisions made well: the right material, a safe setup, a good part, a reliable team, and a promise kept to the customer.
Choose the founder who represents you, give them a name, and name the company you want to build. I will mentor you, but every decision belongs to you.

opening.text: {playerName} signed the lease for {companyName} on an almost-empty garage bay. This is your manufacturing company: one borrowed machine, limited capital, and a chance to build something enormous.
Welcome to the shop. This is your manufacturing company. You will start small, learn every station, and build it into an industrial powerhouse.

opening.kicker: ZACH · YOUR SHOP TEACHER & BUSINESS MENTOR
opening.text: I'm Zach. I'll teach you how real shops win while you choose the customers, equipment, people, and risks.
Your first customer job is on the planning desk. I advise. You own every decision.
Deliver Job 1042 well, reinvest the profit, outgrow this garage, hire a capable team, and build {companyName} into an American manufacturing powerhouse.

first_customer.text: Your first customer job is on the planning desk. I advise. You own every decision.
The customer is hiring your company, not me. Read the requirements, decide whether your shop can keep the promise, and release the work order only when you are ready.

nox_delivery.text: The NOX delivery has arrived with the material certification. Verify the alloy, dimensions, quantity, and paperwork before you release this stock to your process.

first_verified_article.text: The first part is evidence, not inventory. Inspect it before you commit the rest of the material.
Good part. Record what worked so the next operator can repeat it.

first_hire.text: Hire for the work you need now and the capability you will need next. A strong team compounds.
You made the hiring decision. Now give your new teammate a clear station assignment, a safe standard, and enough context to succeed in your company.

garage_graduation.text: Your company has shipped five customer jobs at a passing quality average. The garage proved your process, your judgment, and your promise to customers.
You have outgrown the garage. Now build systems, not just parts.

job_shop_expansion.text: {companyName} delivered five customer jobs and reinvested the profit. You are moving into a real job shop with more CNC capacity, a toolroom, and room for your first hires.
This job shop gives you more capacity and more ways to fail. Standardize the handoffs before volume exposes them.
You have outgrown the garage. Now build systems, not just parts.

plannedSequences.fallback: Implemented Chapter 1 delivery beat uses active founder-overlay shop art and existing pallet-delivery SFX.
Passing first-article milestone is implemented with exact narration; machine result supplies the grade.
First-hire milestone is implemented using the selected founder overlay; candidate portraits remain in the hiring UI.

needs: runtime mechanics and gates listed in campaignPlan

campaignPlan.truthRule: A planned beat is storybook-visible for production planning but must never be presented as playable, voiced, or visually complete.

chapters.title: From Craftsperson to Team
chapters.difficulty: Guided growth · approachable teamwork, quality, and scheduling with forgiving recovery

beats.title: The Team Is the Machine
beats.text: The founder enters the Job Shop and learns that more capacity creates scheduling, tooling, inspection, and handoff risk.

beats.title: Quote, Route, and Staff
beats.text: Quote repeat work, plan its route, hire an operator and programmer, and assign only qualified people.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach lesson on delegation and payroll.

beats.title: Two Machines, One Promise
beats.text: Run VMC and lathe work concurrently without allowing either traveler, tool list, or due date to disappear.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach concurrency and bottleneck guidance.

beats.title: Inspection Is Production
beats.text: Document eight inspections, ship on time, cover payroll, and sustain positive cash while quality rises to a 4.0 average.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach quality-system and cash-flow guidance.

beats.title: The Missed Handoff
beats.text: Recover a late, at-risk job by correcting the handoff and schedule instead of hiding the defect or rushing unsafe work.

beats.title: Job-Shop Owner
beats.text: Ship eight chapter jobs at 4.0 quality, 85 percent on time, with three people, five concurrent completions, eight inspections, and three positive-cash shifts; then move to the Connected Plant.
beats.visualNeeds: Connected Plant arrival and graduation art.

chapters.title: Manage What You Cannot See
chapters.difficulty: Intermediate · Zach-guided telemetry, bottlenecks, maintenance, and multi-shift leadership

beats.title: Signals and Blind Spots
beats.text: The founder enters a 45,000-square-foot plant where no one person can watch every machine or handoff.

beats.title: Connect the Truth
beats.text: Connect eight machines, distinguish running from producing, and learn OEE, downtime, and trustworthy production signals.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach OEE and data-context tutorial.

beats.title: Find the Constraint
beats.text: Schedule machining cells, toolroom, maintenance, quality, and shipping to expose and recover the actual bottleneck.

beats.title: Lead the Other Shift
beats.text: Build clean handoffs and lead eight people across shifts while preserving quality and delivery.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach multi-shift leadership guidance.

beats.title: The Dashboard Was Green
beats.text: Recover a customer-risk event where a healthy-looking metric concealed starvation, blockage, or bad context.

beats.title: Connected-Plant Leader
beats.text: Ship ten chapter jobs at 4.0 quality and 88 percent on time after ten OEE reviews and three root-cause recoveries; then fund the Smart Factory.
beats.visualNeeds: Smart Factory arrival and graduation art.

chapters.title: Standardize and Automate
chapters.difficulty: Advanced · controlled tooling, digital standards, fixtures, cobots, and capability

beats.title: Automate the Standard
beats.text: The founder enters a 120,000-square-foot factory where automation will multiply every good process and every bad one.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach automation principle scene.

beats.title: Control the Tool
beats.text: Preset and control one hundred tools, prove offsets, and eliminate undocumented substitutions.

beats.title: Design the Cell
beats.text: Design fixtures and handoffs, install a safe cobot, and validate capability before increasing rate.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach cell-design and safety guidance.

beats.title: Fourteen Clean Handoffs
beats.text: Sustain controlled digital handoffs and improve flow without hiding defects or bypassing inspection.

beats.title: Fast Scrap
beats.text: Stop an automated cell that is multiplying a weak process, find the cause, and revalidate it safely.

beats.title: Smart-Factory Architect
beats.text: Ship twelve jobs at 4.2 quality and 90 percent on time with three validated automated cells and five measured improvements; then open the Lights-Out Complex.

chapters.title: Reliability at Industrial Scale
chapters.difficulty: Expert · unattended production, robotics, material flow, maintenance, and recovery

beats.title: Trust the Night
beats.text: The founder enters a 350,000-square-foot complex where unattended production is earned through preparation, not hope.

beats.title: Make It Unattended-Ready
beats.text: Prove tool life, chip control, gauging, material supply, and safe fault behavior before the team leaves.

beats.title: Coordinate the Fleet
beats.text: Coordinate robots and AMRs through five hundred cycles without starving machines, blocking aisles, or losing material identity.

beats.title: Ten Reliable Nights
beats.text: Sustain preventive maintenance, less than three percent scrap, and ten successful unattended nights.

beats.title: The 2 A.M. Call
beats.text: Execute five recovery drills and respond to a night fault without creating a safety, quality, or traceability escape.

beats.title: Lights-Out Operations Leader
beats.text: Ship fourteen jobs at 4.3 quality and 92 percent on time after ten reliable nights and five recovery drills; then build the American Titan Campus.
beats.visualNeeds: Titan Campus arrival and graduation art.

chapters.title: Build the Institution
chapters.difficulty: Titan · multi-building strategy, certified programs, leaders, apprentices, and legacy

beats.title: Build Beyond Yourself
beats.text: The founder opens a million-square-foot manufacturing campus serving aerospace, energy, robotics, and American industry.

beats.title: Win the Program
beats.text: Price, negotiate, and execute strategic programs while protecting aerospace traceability and customer trust.

beats.title: Leaders, Not Helpers
beats.text: Develop six department leaders who can make good decisions without waiting for the founder at every station.

beats.title: The Apprentice Academy
beats.text: Launch an academy, transfer standards and judgment, and graduate three apprentices into meaningful factory roles.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach teaching and legacy guidance.

beats.title: The Promise at Scale
beats.text: Resolve a campus-wide program crisis without sacrificing people, traceability, quality, or the institution's future.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach final crisis scene.

beats.title: American Manufacturing Titan
beats.text: Complete ten certified program jobs, three long-term agreements, six department leaders, three apprentice graduations, and eight profitable weeks at 4.5 quality and 95 percent on time.
beats.visualNeeds: Final campus panorama, department leaders, apprentices, and mentor-legacy art.
beats.audioNeeds: New approved Zach finale and player legacy narration.
